notes: Discriminating case = eval-0 (kawaii-feature-deck): with_skill 100% (7/7) vs baseline 86% (6/7). The one baseline failure is the callout check — the pre-change kawaii template has NO callout class, so the baseline fell back to blockquote 'verdict pills' + an unstyled code fence. That gap is exactly what this change adds.

Regression guard = eval-1 (handbook-svg-diagram): BOTH configs 100% (6/6), including 'no ```d2 leaked' and 'hand-written SVG embedded'. Confirms removing the d2 pipeline did not break handbook SVG embedding — pandoc's native rsvg-convert path renders the figure in both versions.

Most assertions are NON-discriminating (pass in both configs): PDF builds, page size, SVG embed, title, glossary. They verify 'nothing broke', not 'skill adds value'. Only the callout assertion separates the versions because the new kawaii features are ADDITIVE — a baseline that doesn't know them simply omits them instead of failing. The real value (callouts, styled code blocks, richer SVG hero/scene art, design polish) is QUALITATIVE — judge it in the Outputs tab.

Assertion fix mid-eval: 'title in PDF text' was a false-negative — the handbook cover wraps 'Choosing a Vector / Database' across two lines so pdftotext split it. Grader now compares whitespace-normalized text; both eval-1 runs then passed. The title was visually present throughout.
Cost/time: with_skill was cheaper AND faster on the kawaii case (148s / 49k tok vs baseline 184s / 70k) and comparable on handbook (169s/40k vs 174s/44k) — the new affordances added no overhead.
Only 1 run per configuration, so pass rates have no variance estimate. To get a confidence signal, expand to 3 runs/config in a later iteration.
